Date: 25th March, 2026.
Reference: - 1) Department of Finance, Government Circular No.
Dated April 7, 2025, dated January 29, 2026, and dated March 12, 2026.
2) Regional Water Conservation Officer, Soil and Water Conservation Department, Nagpur Office
Go to letter No. 215 / Lesha, dated February 14, 2025
The Proposal: -
For works in non-tribal areas under Water Resources Cement Dams (Checkdams) Programme
Demand No. ZH-5, account title 4402 2799 in the year 2025-26 below
A fund of Rs 100.00 crore was budgeted. Now the reference of the Finance Department is No. 1
From the funds made available under the revised estimate limit as per the circulars herein.
Reference No. 2 The following decision has been taken on the fund demand proposal here.
The Government's Decisions:
Arvi, Astti and Karanja taluks of Wardha district are at reference no. 2 in the proposal.
Total amount of Rs. for payment of pending payments for works done for deepening and widening of drains
197.58 Lakhs (literal Rs.1 crore, ninety-nine lakh, ninety-eight thousand only)
The approval of the Government is hereby being granted for distribution of: -
The aforesaid funds are being disbursed subject to the following conditions and conditions: -
a) Water Conservation Corporation, District for the above deepening and widening works
If funds have been received from the Planning Committee or other source, this
The funds being distributed should not be withdrawn or distributed,
b) Administrative approval as well as revised administrative approval as approved by the Government.
Funds should not be spent in excess of the cost.
c) If the above works do not require funds, the funds are reciprocal in any case.
Should not be distributed for other projects / works,
d) If for any reason the fund remains unfilled, the fund is immediately returned to the Government.
to be done,
e) Reference No. 1 in the circular here.
Follow the guidelines strictly.
f) Payments to ensure quality of work and compliance report.
submitted to the government.
3. The amount disbursed by it to the Regional Water Conservation Officer, Mrid and
The Water Conservation Department, Nagpur has taken over the respective district water conservation under its jurisdiction.
It should be delivered to the authorities quickly.
4. The expenditure on this should be covered under the following headings and the year 2025-
26 to be subtracted from the sanctioned provision for this financial year.
Demand No. ZH-5,
4402 - Capital expenditure on soil and water conservation,
102 - Soil conservation,
(02) (02) Cement Dam (Check Dam) Programme (4402 2799)
53, Major constructions
5. Submitting utilization certificates of the above expenses to the Accountant General's office.
Submit copies of its response as well as the project completion certificate to the government in the prescribed manner.
Do it.

Officer, Soil and Water Conservation Department, Nagpur and Additional Commissioner Water Conservation, Mrid.
and Water Conservation, Regional Area, Nagpur will be the coordinating authority for these matters.
